stories.title: OpenAI acquires Cirrus Labs
stories.kit_take: This is agent infrastructure acquisition — OpenAI needs Cirrus's proven ephemeral execution environments and sandboxed primitives that took 9 years to perfect, not talent or tooling.
stories.dean_take: OpenAI is assembling the full coding stack quietly, piece by piece, and Cirrus is the latest brick in that infrastructure.

stories.title: Linux kernel adds AI coding assistant documentation
stories.kit_take: The kernel's policy is the most pragmatic AI acceptance framework written this year — tool as assistant, human as author, with zero exceptions and full accountability.
stories.dean_take: This conservative codebase just wrote the most pragmatic AI policy, and when Linux documents something, enterprise open source policies will cite it within a year.

stories.title: OpenAI Academy launches training guides
stories.kit_take: This is enterprise land-and-expand strategy, and the guides are thin SEO content, but the stranger signal is brands telling copywriters to avoid em dashes and metaphors to not look AI-written.
stories.dean_take: OpenAI is late to this training layer that Microsoft and Google have been executing for months — free content to create organizational dependency then convert at company level.

predictions: Dean: Enterprise open source policies will be citing Linux's AI documentation within a year
key_themes: Human accountability vs. tool capability gap
AI reshaping human writing habits and stylistic choices
Pragmatic vs. restrictive approaches to AI integration
episodes.story_to_watch: OpenAI's quiet assembly of the full autonomous coding stack and how Linux kernel standards become the template for enterprise AI adoption
